title: Unifying CRM Data into Real-Time Customer Engagement Infrastructure
subtitle: Translating CRM events into real-time customer signals to power lifecycle engagement at scale
role: Customer Data Synchronization Across Core Business Platforms

isBrandedMetric.title: CORE CRM MODULES INTEGRATED
isBrandedMetric.description: Enabled unified customer engagement by synchronizing core CRM modules in real time.

overview: Truva, a fast-scaling real estate platform, operates across a complex ecosystem of buyers, sellers, and channel partners, with critical customer and operational data distributed across multiple CRM modules including Leads, Sellers, Properties, and Channel Partners. This set up prevented the business from creating a unified customer view and triggering timely, context-driven engagement.

Attributics partnered with Truva to build a scalable, reliable data synchronization framework between the CRM and Netcore Cloud using a real-time, event-driven architecture on AWS. The objective was to enable seamless, real-time movement of customer and partner data across systems while preserving business context across Buyer, Seller, and Channel Partner journeys. Beyond integration, the broader goal was to establish a unified engagement foundation that improved workflow efficiency, enabled consistent communication, and supported future scale without increasing operational complexity for internal teams.

challenge.subtitle: Complex Multi-Entity Synchronization
challenge.description: Truva’s engagement model spanned multiple stakeholder groups, each with distinct data structures, lifecycle states, and communication needs. This created the need for a synchronization framework capable of supporting differentiated data logic while maintaining consistency across all engagement environments.

challenge.subtitle: Operational Dependency on Business Events
challenge.description: Critical business actions within the CRM such as property changes and acquisition stage updates needed to translate into timely and accurate downstream engagement actions. Ensuring these dependencies were captured reliably was essential to maintaining continuity between CRM activity and customer communication.

challenge.subtitle: Need for Scale and System Resilience
challenge.description: As platform activity and lead volumes increased, the integration had to remain stable, responsive, and operationally reliable. Avoiding duplication, preventing sync failures, and sustaining performance under growing API demand were central to building an architecture that could support long-term growth.

solution.description: Attributics designed a scalable, event-driven integration framework connecting the CRM with Netcore Cloud through AWS serverless services. The solution enabled real-time synchronization of customer and partner data across Truva’s Buyer, Seller, and Channel Partner journeys, ensuring critical CRM actions could reliably trigger downstream engagement workflows.

blocks.text: Attributics designed a scalable, event-driven integration framework connecting the CRM with Netcore Cloud through AWS serverless services. The solution enabled real-time synchronization of customer and partner data across Truva’s Buyer, Seller, and Channel Partner journeys, ensuring critical CRM actions could reliably trigger downstream engagement workflows.

solution.description: Webhook notifications from CRM modules such as Leads, Properties, Sellers, and Channel Partners were captured through AWS API Gateway and routed into Amazon SQS for asynchronous and reliable message handling. AWS Lambda (Node.js) then consumed these messages, transformed CRM payloads into Netcore-compatible schemas, and used a dynamic userType flag to apply the correct sync logic for each panel.

blocks.text: Webhook notifications from CRM modules such as Leads, Properties, Sellers, and Channel Partners were captured through AWS API Gateway and routed into Amazon SQS for asynchronous and reliable message handling. AWS Lambda (Node.js) then consumed these messages, transformed CRM payloads into Netcore-compatible schemas, and used a dynamic userType flag to apply the correct sync logic for each panel.

solution.description: To ensure data quality and operational resilience, Zod validation was applied within Lambda, webhook payloads were backed up in Amazon S3, CloudWatch was used for performance and queue monitoring, and Sentry provided real-time error tracking. Within the CRM, Deluge functions were attached to custom workflows so that business events such as lead qualification changes, acquisition stage movement, or property status updates could automatically trigger the appropriate sync actions.

blocks.text: To ensure data quality and operational resilience, Zod validation was applied within Lambda, webhook payloads were backed up in Amazon S3, CloudWatch was used for performance and queue monitoring, and Sentry provided real-time error tracking. Within the CRM, Deluge functions were attached to custom workflows so that business events such as lead qualification changes, acquisition stage movement, or property status updates could automatically trigger the appropriate sync actions.

process.title: Audit & Integration Mapping
process.description: The project began with a detailed review of Truva’s CRM structure, key business modules, workflow dependencies, and Netcore panel requirements. This helped define how data needed to move across systems while preserving stakeholder context.

process.title: Architecture Design
process.description: A real-time, event-driven integration model was established using AWS services to support scalability, reliability, and operational flexibility. The framework was designed to manage differentiated logic across multiple user groups without creating siloed workflows.

process.title: Workflow & Sync Implementation
process.description: Custom Deluge functions were configured within the CRM to trigger synchronization events based on business actions. These events were routed through API Gateway, queued in SQS, transformed in Lambda, and delivered into the appropriate Netcore panels.

process.title: Validation, Monitoring & Resilience Setup
process.description: Schema validation, payload backup, queue retry logic, error handling, and monitoring layers were implemented to ensure data quality, auditability, and reliable performance under scale.

process.title: Testing & Operational Readiness
process.description: The full sync framework was validated across modules and user journeys to confirm that business events were triggering the right downstream actions accurately and consistently before production use.

results.metric: Core CRM Modules Unified into 1 Real-Time Data Layer
Real-Time Data Synchronization Across Engagement Systems
Data Loss Achieved with Event-Driven Architecture

conclusion: This integration marked a critical step in Truva’s data modernization journey, transforming fragmented systems into a unified, real-time engagement foundation.

By connecting CRM and engagement platforms through an event-driven architecture, Truva enabled reliable data flow, automated lifecycle triggers, and improved speed of execution, creating a scalable ecosystem for future growth.
disclaimer: This case study is presented by Attributics to showcase our project capabilities and approach. The content is not intended as an official representation on behalf of the client or its affiliated entities. To protect confidentiality, certain names, data, and business-sensitive details may have been anonymized or generalized. The case study will be updated or removed upon client request.
